topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The doorbell rang, a sharp, insistent sound that made me jump. I peered through the peephole, my heart giving a sudden, excited thump against my ribs. It was a package. A large, brown box sitting squarely on my welcome mat. I fumbled with the deadbolt, my hands trembling slightly as I pulled the door open. Inside, nestled in crumpled packing paper, was a collection of vintage vinyl records—exactly what I’d been searching for. A wide, unrestrained smile stretched across my face, the kind that makes your cheeks ache. "Oh, you wonderful soul," I breathed, clutching the box to my chest, already planning where to place the first record.

[story 2]

I was sorting through the junk mail when the delivery arrived. A plain cardboard box, addressed to the previous occupant, was left on my stoop. Curiosity immediately took hold. I wrestled the door open, my posture instantly straightening, a bright energy bubbling up inside me. When I lifted the lid, the contents were a box of rare, first-edition books. My eyes widened, and I let out a soft, breathy laugh. "No way," I muttered to myself, running a finger over the embossed lettering on the spine of one volume. The sheer unexpectedness of it sent a thrill down my spine, making my whole body feel light and buoyant.

[story 3]

It was late afternoon, and I was watering my balcony plants when the delivery truck pulled up. A package, clearly marked with an old address, was placed by the railing. I walked over, my steps quickening with anticipation. As I carefully untied the string, the contents revealed themselves: a set of antique botanical sketches I’d admired online for months. A genuine, unrestrained grin spread across my face, pulling at the corners of my mouth. I turned the package over in my hands, a sense of pure, unadulterated delight washing over me, making me want to shout with happiness. "This is incredible," I whispered, shaking my head slightly in disbelief.
